props.topic: HUMANITARIANS AI · Build a Variational Floor Plot with Claude Code

beats.narration_text: This is Kore, in for Humanitarians AI. Humanitarians AI — Claude Code, Manim, and the physics, taken apart.

props.command: Build a Variational Floor Plot with Claude Code
props.segment: Build a Variational Floor Plot with Claude Code

beats.narration_text: Here is why Build a Variational Floor Plot with Claude Code belongs in your working knowledge. The systems, policies, and tools shaped by it affect decisions at your level — whether or not you understand the mechanism. Knowing the underlying logic gives you the frame; missing it means reasoning from output with no access to the inputs.

props.command: Build a Variational Floor Plot with Claude Code
props.segment: Build a Variational Floor Plot with Claude Code

beats.narration_text: The prompt. Claude Code terminal: claude — plot the helium variational energy as a function of the effective nuclear charge Z-star. Use the formula: E of Z-star equals Z-star-squared minus two times Z times Z-star plus five eighths times Z-star, all in electron-volts scaled from the hydrogen ground state. Show Z-star from one to three. Mark the minimum and the true ground state at minus seventy-nine electron-volts. Specify: the minimum at Z-star equals one-point-six-eight-seven-five, the energy there, and the bound that every trial is above the true floor.

props.command:
claude "plot helium variational energy E(Z*) vs Z*:
  E(Z*) = 27.2 eV × [Z*² - 2×2×Z* + (5/8)×Z*]
  Z* ∈ [1, 3]
  mark minimum at Z*=27/16=1.6875
  mark true E₀=-79.0 eV (NIST floor)
  show: every trial > floor"

beats.narration_text: The CLI writes it. Read before running. Two checks. First: the minimum. The variational energy reaches its lowest value at Z-star equals twenty-seven sixteenths — approximately one-point-six-eight-seven-five. The code should find this by taking the derivative and setting it to zero. If the minimum is at Z-star equals two, the code used the bare nuclear charge instead of the optimized one. Second: the energy at the minimum. It should read minus seventy-seven-point-five electron-volts. The true ground state is minus seventy-nine. Every trial is above the floor. Thirty seconds.

props.code:
# variational.py  —  Claude Code output
import numpy as np

# Helium Z=2; variational trial: hydrogen-like with Z*
# E(Z*) = 27.2 eV × [Z*² − 2×2×Z* + (5/8)×Z*]
# = 27.2 × [Z*² − (27/8)Z*]  (using He: 2Z=4 → corrected)

Zstar = np.linspace(1, 3, 300)
# Full expression with screening correction:
E = 27.2 * (Zstar**2 - 2*2*Zstar + (5/8)*Zstar)
# Minimum: dE/dZ* = 0 → Z* = 2 - 5/16 = 27/16 = 1.6875
# E_min ≈ -77.5 eV  (above true E₀ = -79.0 eV)
props.title: variational.py

beats.narration_text: Run it. Two checks. First: the teal curve minimum. The curve dips to minus seventy-seven-point-five electron-volts at Z-star equals one-point-six-eight-seven-five. Anything below two is partially screened. Second: the crimson floor. The dashed line at minus seventy-nine electron-volts is the true helium ground state from NIST. The teal curve never crosses it. It touches the floor from above. The variational principle guarantees that. Every guess is too high. The quantum chemistry of the periodic table is built on that floor.

Iterate: claude — update variational dot py — add a second curve for a better trial state using a two-parameter wave function. Lower minimum, still above the floor. One diff, one re-run. Prompt, read, run, check, change. The variational floor was the vehicle. The loop is the takeaway.

props.command:
claude "update variational.py:
  → add two-param trial: E₂ ≈ -77.8 eV
  → show both curves above E₀=-79.0 eV floor"

beats.narration_text: Humanitarians AI. Brutalist and Educational A-I. On YouTube, at Humanitarians AI. Humanitarians AI dot com.
